content.problemStatement:
Chef has an exam which will start exactly MMM minutes from now. However, instead of preparing for his exam, Chef started watching Season-111 of Superchef. Season-111 has NNN episodes, and the duration of **each** episode is KKK minutes.

Will Chef be able to finish watching Season-111 **strictly before** the exam starts?

Note:\textbf{Note:}Note: Please read the explanations of the sample test cases carefully.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. TTT test cases then follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains 333 space separated integers MMM, NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output on one line YES if it is possible to finish Season-1 **strictly before** the exam starts, or NO if it is not possible to do so.


Output is case insensitive, which means that "yes", "Yes", "YEs", "no", "nO" - all such strings will be acceptable.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers M, N and K — the minutes until the exam starts, the number of episodes, and the duration of each episode in minutes.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if Chef can finish watching all episodes strictly before the exam starts, otherwise output "NO". The output is case‑insensitive.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: total = 1*10 = 10, which is not less than M=10 → NO.\nTest case 2: total = 2*10 = 20 < 25 → YES.\nTest case 3: total = 2*10 = 20 > 15 → NO.
First case: total = 10*10 = 100 equals M, so Chef cannot finish strictly before → NO.\nSecond case: total = 100 < 101 → YES.
total = 1*1 = 1 equals M, therefore not strictly before → NO.

solution.approach: For each test case compute the total watching time total = N * K. If total < M, Chef finishes strictly before the exam, so print YES; otherwise print NO. Multiplication fits in 64‑bit signed integer, so use long long (or Python int) to avoid overflow.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        M = int(next(it)); N = int(next(it)); K = int(next(it))
        total = N * K
        out.append('YES' if total < M else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
